_comment: ISOLATION EXPERIMENT B (control) — is it the |u| NONLINEARITY or the reaction MAGNITUDE/heterogeneity? Same corner as the full-Forchheimer fold (Re=1e5, alpha_0=0.1, ASGS, P1/P1, meshes [10,20,40]) but with a LINEAR reaction: sigma_nonlinear=0, sigma_linear bumped to 0.4944 so the effective Damkohler MATCHES the full case (Da_eff = 0.4944*((1-0.1)/0.1)^2 = 0.4944*81 = 40.05, versus full Forchheimer 0.3*81 + 1.75*9 = 40.05). Same encoding path, same heterogeneous alpha(x) field, same magnitude — only the velocity-dependent b(alpha)|u| term is removed. If THIS converges while full Forchheimer at alpha=0.1 FOLDS, the |u| nonlinearity (dsigma/du basin-shrink) is the culprit; if it also folds, it is heterogeneity/magnitude, not nonlinearity. Companion: isolation_alphasweep.json.
